title: T-007 — Register 6 P1 DSL rules in 02-Settings rules registry (MNT §8.1)
prompt:
# T-007 — Register maintenance DSL rules

PRD: docs/prd/13-MAINTENANCE-PRD.md §8.1, §8.2 (mwo_state_machine_v1 definition), §11.1-§11.6 (V-MNT validations)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Register the 6 P1-active maintenance DSL rules into the 02-Settings rules registry (`rule_definitions` table from Foundation T-018/T-035) so they can be enabled/disabled per tenant. The `oee_maintenance_trigger_v1` rule (P2) is registered as `status='P2_stub'` (consumer-only stub).

## Pre-flight
Verify `rule_definitions` schema exists in `packages/db/src/schema/settings/rules.ts` (Foundation T-018 / 02-Settings §7); read its column shape (rule_id, rule_type, definition_yaml, status, active, owner_module).

## Implementation contract
1. Seed migration `packages/db/migrations/NNNN_maintenance_rules_seed.sql` INSERTing 7 rows into `rule_definitions` (6 P1 active + 1 P2 stub):
   - `mwo_state_machine_v1` (workflow_as_data, status=P1_active) — paste the §8.2 YAML verbatim into `definition_yaml`.
   - `pm_schedule_due_engine_v1` (cascading, P1_active).
   - `calibration_expiry_alert_v1` (cascading, P1_active).
   - `spare_parts_reorder_alert_v1` (gate, P1_active).
   - `sanitation_allergen_gate_v1` (gate, P1_active).
   - `loto_pre_execution_gate_v1` (gate, P1_active).
   - `oee_maintenance_trigger_v1` (gate+workflow, status=P2_stub, owner_module='15-OEE', consumer_module='13-MAINTENANCE').
2. Each rule row carries `org_id = NULL` (system-level rule) per Foundation rule registry convention; tenant enable/disable is via `tenant_rule_overrides` (out of scope).
3. Migration must be idempotent: `ON CONFLICT (rule_id) DO NOTHING` (rules already registered by other modules' migrations).
4. Vitest: query `rule_definitions` after migration; assert presence of all 7 rule_ids with correct status; assert `mwo_state_machine_v1.definition_yaml` parses as YAML and contains the 6 states + guards.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/NNNN_maintenance_rules_seed.sql [create]
- packages/db/__tests__/maintenance-rules-seed.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given migration runs, when SELECT FROM rule_definitions WHERE rule_id LIKE 'mwo_%' OR rule_id IN (…6 maintenance rules…), then 7 rows are present with correct rule_type/status/owner_module per §8.1.
2. Given `mwo_state_machine_v1.definition_yaml` is read, when parsed as YAML, then it contains states `requested`, `approved`, `open`, `in_progress`, `completed`, `cancelled` with allowed_from/allowed_to/guards arrays matching §8.2.
3. Given migration is re-applied to a populated DB, when executed, then `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` causes no error and no row changes.
4. Given `oee_maintenance_trigger_v1` row, when inspected, then `status='P2_stub'` and `owner_module='15-OEE'` and `consumer_module='13-MAINTENANCE'`.

## Test strategy
- RED: maintenance-rules-seed.test.ts asserting four ACs before migration exists.
- Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test maintenance-rules-seed`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not invent new columns in `rule_definitions` — use the columns Foundation T-018/02-Settings §7 already defines.
- Do not enable `oee_maintenance_trigger_v1` for P1 — it is feature-flagged (`maintenance_triggers_enabled` default false per D-MNT-11).
- Do not paraphrase the §8.2 YAML — paste verbatim; the rule engine T-035 parses it.
- Do not seed tenant-level overrides — that is a Settings task.

pipeline_inputs.description: Seed 6 P1-active + 1 P2-stub maintenance DSL rules into rule_definitions registry per PRD §8.1.
pipeline_inputs.details: Idempotent ON CONFLICT seed. YAML of mwo_state_machine_v1 pasted verbatim from §8.2. Cross-module note: oee_maintenance_trigger_v1 owner=15-OEE, consumer=13-MAINTENANCE — actual rule engine evaluation lives in 15-OEE later. loto_pre_execution_gate_v1 and calibration_expiry_alert_v1 rules consume e-sign primitive T-124 at execution time — signEvent/dualSign with replay nonce + paired audit (retention='security').
acceptance_criteria: Given migration runs, when SELECT FROM rule_definitions for maintenance rules, then 7 rows are present with correct rule_type/status/owner_module per §8.1
Given mwo_state_machine_v1.definition_yaml is parsed, when inspected, then it contains 6 states with allowed_from/allowed_to/guards matching §8.2
Given migration is re-applied, when executed, then ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING causes no error
Given oee_maintenance_trigger_v1 row, when inspected, then status='P2_stub' and owner_module='15-OEE' and consumer_module='13-MAINTENANCE'
test_strategy: Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test maintenance-rules-seed`
